market_summary: S&P 500 hit fresh all-time record 7,137.90 on April 22 driven by Trump's indefinite Iran ceasefire extension and strong earnings season (Tesla beat, 3 of 4 S&P companies exceeding expectations). S&P pulled back -0.41% on April 23 to 7,108.40; Nasdaq retreated to 24,438.50 (-0.89%) on tech selling and oil price uncertainty. Week-to-date S&P +0.63% from April 17 close. Iran attacked 3 commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz on April 22 — the same day the ceasefire was extended — confirming the conflict is unresolved. Hormuz remains closed with $1M+ tolls per vessel and US naval blockade ongoing. Gold under pressure (-8.3% over 30 days), significant ETF outflows in March (largest since 2013), Morgan Stanley downgraded year-end gold target from $5,700 to $5,200/oz. AVGO maintains highest-conviction new-position candidate status (92% analyst Buy/Outperform) but NAV gate ($5,000) remains unmet at $4,305.76.
macro_themes: 1. IRAN/HORMUZ (DOMINANT): Despite Trump's indefinite ceasefire extension on April 22, Iran attacked 3 commercial ships the same day. Hormuz remains closed, $1M+ vessel tolls, US naval blockade ongoing. GLD 4-week invalidation clock has NOT started. 2. INFLATION: March 2026 CPI confirmed +3.3% YoY / +0.9% MoM — largest monthly gasoline spike since 1967 (+21.2%). Core CPI +2.6% YoY. April CPI due May 12, projected to peak near 4% before declining toward 3% if Hormuz resolves. 3. FED: FOMC April 28-29 expected to hold at 3.50-3.75% ('easy call' per JPMorgan). Policymakers signal 1 cut in 2026 but JPM forecasts NO cuts with possible 25bps hike Q3 2027. U03A stage-down trigger unlikely to fire. 4. AI CAPEX SUPERCYCLE: NVDA $1T+ Blackwell+Vera Rubin backlog confirmed (doubled YoY). Vera Rubin in full production on H2 2026 track. AMZN Q1 earnings April 29 — AWS acceleration to 28-30% expected. 5. SECTOR ROTATION: Capital rotating from Energy (Q1 leader losing momentum) back into tech and cyclicals. 'Great Rotation' out of tech has reversed. Tech leadership re-asserting at all-time highs despite April 23 pullback. 6. TARIFFS: 90-day pause on elevated tariff rates (ex-China 125%) continues with no new escalation. Trump threatened 50% tariff on countries supplying military weapons to Iran.
portfolio_health: Grade: B (80/100). NAV $4,305.76. Week return +0.41% vs S&P 500 +0.63% = -0.22pp underperformance — entirely attributable to 8.44% cash drag with S&P at all-time highs. Underlying position performance positive. CSPX fully at target (38.94% of holdings NAV). ASML exit complete. YTD from April 4 seed ($4,191.00): +2.74%. WHT YTD: $3.33 withheld (MA dividend only) — structurally excellent. Key pending catalysts: FOMC April 28-29, AMZN earnings April 29, MA earnings April 30, May 3 cash deployment hard deadline. GLD WATCH flag elevated — $431.04 with only 2.6% buffer above $420 trim trigger (tightened from 3.6% yesterday). NVDA STRONGEST position at +9.71% unrealized. MA -2.12% unrealized; pre-earnings consolidation, not thesis signal.

new_opportunities.rationale: Broadcom is the highest-conviction new-position candidate — AI custom silicon leadership via Google/Meta hyperscaler partnerships through 2029-2031, $73B AI backlog targeting $100B+ AI revenue by FY2027, VMware networking dominance. AI chip revenue projected to grow 100%+ in 2026. 92% analyst Buy/Outperform consensus, median price target $458 (up to $525 Street high). Negligible dividend = 0% effective WHT. Complementary to NVDA (custom XPUs vs standard GPUs). Gate: NAV must exceed $5,000 AND CSPX gap fully closed. Current NAV $4,305.76 is $694 below threshold — do NOT buy yet.

action_items: 1. URGENT — This week: Deploy ~$213.29 into CSPX at market (April 25 Friday or April 28 Monday open at latest). May 3 hard deadline is 9 days away. Execute unconditionally.
2. CRITICAL — April 28-29 FOMC: If dovish signal (explicit rate cut window), trim U03A -$100 and deploy into CSPX immediately. If hold-unchanged or hawkish: no U03A action.

3. CRITICAL — April 29 (5:30 PM ET) AMZN Q1 earnings: ADD $50-75 if AWS growth >25% AND Q1 EBIT >$20B AND Q2 guidance maintained or raised. HOLD if in-line. No exit unless EBIT below $16.5B floor with Q2 guide-down.
4. CRITICAL — April 30 MA Q1 earnings: ADD $100 if cross-border volume >14% AND VAS growth >20% AND guidance maintained or raised. HOLD if in-line. Evaluate REDUCE only on material miss + guide-down.
5. HARD DEADLINE — May 3: Deploy all remaining deployable cash into CSPX unconditionally. If MA add executed: deploy ~$163; if not: deploy ~$263. Retain $50 minimum buffer. No extensions.

6. DAILY MONITOR — GLD $420 alert: GLD at $431.04, only 2.6% above trim trigger. If daily close below $420: trim 25% of GLD (~$108) and redeploy into CSPX immediately. Trigger could fire within 1-3 sessions.
7. WATCH — NVDA earnings date: Verify Q1 FY2027 earnings date — May 20 vs May 27 discrepancy noted between sources. Prior thesis corrected to May 27. Confirm before that date.
